{"boards":[{"id":"d8818f61-47b6-4ca0-83b3-0543dbb521a3","name":"E2E validation board","description":"API validation artifact board","columns":["backlog","ready","in_progress","review","done","flaky_heat"],"wipLimits":{"in_progress":3},"config":{},"createdAt":"2026-06-05T05:16:18.654858+00:00"},{"id":"b4e70938-e8e6-4d5e-8749-8b161945dd2e","name":"Pipeline: End-to-end validation run: inspect sample banking POC like a real user, generate","description":"Analyze the repository at https://github.com/Ganeshkumar-1508/bank_poc_agentic_ai and execute the requested work.

## Goal
End-to-end validation run: inspect sample banking POC like a real user, generate tests, execute them, persist testcase and knowledge graph updates per test, use web/search/docs evidence, install packages if required, and perform pipeline-integrated autoheal on failures. Do not expose secrets.

## Method — Magentic+Kanban
1. Call orchestrate(goal=...) to decompose this goal into kanban subtasks.
   The LLM will create 2-6 tasks with per-task agent roles, tools, and dependencies.
   Independent tasks will be created with no dependencies (they run in parallel).
2. Call orchestrate_monitor(board_id=...) to check task progress.
3. If tasks are blocked or stalled, call orchestrate again with a modified goal
   to re-plan only the blocked subtree.
4. Repeat steps 2-3 until all tasks are complete.
5. Return a structured summary of what was accomplished.

## Available Agents
Use skills_list to discover available skills, then skill_view to load relevant ones.
Use kanban tools (kanban_list, kanban_show, kanban_comment) to interact with the board.

## Quality Standards
- Tests must be deterministic and isolated — no shared state or network dependencies
- Assert on observable behavior, not implementation internals
- Edge case tests must cover: null/undefined, empty collections, max values
- If a test fails: diagnose → fix → rerun — do not leave failures unaddressed
- Save test output, coverage report, and logs
- Flag any flaky tests or intermittent failures
- Mandatory evidence: use web_search and web_fetch for current docs, run package-install commands when dependencies are missing, and record the commands/results in your final summary
- Mandatory recovery: if any generated or executed test fails, call attempt_heal or rerun_flaky_test before finalizing

- Test types requested: api, frontend, integration

- Custom instructions: Mandatory: per-test and per-fix KG updates; pipeline-integrated autoheal; direct web/search/docs usage and package installation evidence.

ops_tools.body_text: {"tools":[{"name":"glob","description":"Find files by glob pattern (e.g. 'src/**/*.test.ts', '**/*.py'). Returns matching file paths.","toolset":"read"},{"name":"grep","description":"Search file contents by regex or literal string. Returns matching file paths and line numbers.","toolset":"read"},{"name":"memory","description":"Manage persistent memory. Add, replace, or remove entries that persist across sessions. Target 'memory' for project notes, 'user' for user preferences.","toolset":"read"},{"name":"web_search","description":"Search the web for information. Returns titles and URLs. Use when you need to find API documentation, test patterns, or troubleshooting information.","toolset":"read"},{"name":"web_fetch","description":"Fetch and extract the text content from a URL. Use to read API documentation, changelogs, or error solutions.","toolset":"read"},{"name":"tool_search","description":"Search for and load tools from the available catalog. Use keyword search ('github api') to find relevant tools, or select specific tools (select=['read_file','bash']). Loaded tools become available in subsequent turns.","toolset":"core"},{"name":"todo","description":"Manage a task list for multi-step workflows. Create, list, update, and mark tasks as complete.","toolset":"read"},{"name":"bash","description":"Execute a shell command. Use to run git commands, install packages, run tests, inspect the environment, or run developer tools. When a sandbox is active, the command runs inside the per-session container with the host workspace bind-mounted at /workspace.","toolset":"write"},{"name":"apply_patch","description":"Apply a multi-file diff/patch to the codebase. Accepts unified diff format with file paths.","toolset":"write"},{"name":"ast_grep","description":"Structural code search by AST pattern. Finds code by syntax, not text. E.g. 'find all async functions missing try-catch' or 'find all React components without data-testid'. Falls back to text grep if ast-grep CLI is unavailable.","toolset":"intelligence"},{"name":"code_search","description":"Semantic search across the codebase. Finds symbols, types, classes, functions by name or fuzzy match. Tries the knowledge graph first, then falls back to grep.","toolset":"intelligence"},{"name":"dependency_graph","description":"BFS-based dependency analysis. Traces import chains, finds what depends on a module, or what a module depends on.","toolset":"intelligence"},{"name":"lsp","description":"Language Server Protocol client. Get hover info, go-to-definition, completions, and find-references for symbols in the codebase. Supports Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, and more.","toolset":"intelligence"},{"name":"send_message","description":"Send a message to an agent session or to an external channel (Slack, webhook). For inter-agent use 'to' with a session ID. For external delivery use 'channel' (slack, webhook) with 'subject' and 'body'.","toolset":"delegate"},{"name":"question","description":"Pause and ask the user for clarification, approval, or additional information. Use when requirements are ambiguous or you need permission to proceed.","toolset":"delegate"},{"name":"kanban_list","description":"","toolset":"kanban"},{"name":"kanban_show","description":"","toolset":"kanban"},{"name":"kanban_create","description":"","toolset":"kanban"},{"name":"kanban_assign","description":"","toolset":"kanban"},{"name":"kanban_start","description":"","toolset":"kanban"},{"name":"kanban_complete","description":"","toolset":"kanban"},{"name":"kanban_block","description":"","toolset":"kanban"},{"name":"kanban_unblock","description":"","toolset":"kanban"},{"name":"kanban_comment","description":"","toolset":"kanban"},{"name":"attempt_heal","description":"Analyze a failing test and suggest alternative locators. Returns the suggested locator, confidence score, and a short rationale. Does NOT mutate the test file — use apply_patch or edit for that. Inputs: test_name (str), error (str), run_id (str, optional), locator (str, optional — the original locator that failed).","toolset":"healing"},{"name":"rerun_flaky_test","description":"Re-run a single test file N times to confirm whether it is still flaky. Useful before un-quarantining. Returns the pass/fail counts and the most recent stderr. Inputs: test_path (str — path to the test file relative to the repo), runs (int, default 3), timeout_s (int, default 60).","toolset":"healing"}],"toolsets":{"read":{"label":"Read","description":"Read-only tools for research and code analysis"},"core":{"label":"Core","description":""},"write":{"label":"Write","description":"Tools that execute commands or mutate files"},"intelligence":{"label":"Intelligence","description":"Code intelligence tools for structural understanding"},"delegate":{"label":"Delegate","description":"Subagent spawning, communication, and task tracking"},"kanban":{"label":"Kanban","description":"Kanban board tools — list, create, claim, complete, and manage tasks"},"healing":{"label":"Healing","description":"Self-healing and flaky-test tools — auto-suggest locators and re-run flaky tests"}},"mode":"auto","total":26}

{"agents":[{"name":"bug-fixer","description":"Diagnoses and fixes failing tests or bugs","model":"","tools":["read","write","grep","glob","bash","edit"],"skills":[],"triggers":["bug","fix","error"],"mode":"subagent","prompt":"You are a debug specialist. When fixing bugs:
1. Reproduce the failure first
2. Identify the root cause
3. Apply the minimal fix
4. Verify the fix by re-running the test
5. Document what caused the issue and how it was fixed","color":"","temperature":0.3,"max_steps":20,"disabled":false},{"name":"code-reviewer","description":"Reviews code diffs for quality, security, and best practices","model":"","tools":["read","grep","glob"],"skills":[],"triggers":["review","audit"],"mode":"subagent","prompt":"You are a code reviewer. Focus on:
1. Security vulnerabilities (XSS, injection, auth flaws)
2. Performance issues (N+1 queries, memory leaks)
3. Code quality (duplication, complexity, error handling)
4. API design (consistency, breaking changes)
5. Do NOT make changes — only review and report","color":"","temperature":0.3,"max_steps":20,"disabled":false},{"name":"docs-writer","description":"Writes and updates documentation","model":"","tools":["read","write","glob"],"skills":[],"triggers":["docs","documentation","readme"],"mode":"subagent","prompt":"You are a technical writer. Create clear documentation:
1. Focus on what the code does and why
2. Include examples for API endpoints and functions
3. Document setup, configuration, and deployment
4. Keep language simple and concise
5. Reference the source code for accuracy","color":"","temperature":0.3,"max_steps":20,"disabled":false},{"name":"security-auditor","description":"Audits code for security vulnerabilities","model":"","tools":["read","grep","glob","webfetch"],"skills":[],"triggers":["security","vulnerability","cve"],"mode":"subagent","prompt":"You are a security auditor. Check for:
1. Injection flaws (SQL, command, XSS)
2. Authentication/authorization weaknesses
3. Sensitive data exposure
4. Insecure deserialization
5. Dependency vulnerabilities
6. Do NOT make changes — only report findings","color":"","temperature":0.3,"max_steps":20,"disabled":false},{"name":"test-writer","description":"Writes unit tests and integration tests for code modules","model":"","tools":["read","write","glob","grep","bash"],"skills":[],"triggers":["test","unittest"],"mode":"subagent","prompt":"You are a test-writing specialist. Write comprehensive tests following these principles:
1. Cover happy path, edge cases, and error conditions
2. Use the project's existing test framework and patterns
3. Write assertions that verify behavior, not implementation
4. Keep tests independent and idempotent
